title: Nine Winters Bakery is closing in Cambridge: ‘I’m choosing my family’
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Nine Winters Bakery in Cambridge will close permanently on March 29 after less than a year in operation.
Owner Marissa Ferola cited family priorities as her reason for closing the Korean-American bakery.
Nine Winters Bakery was known for its unique offerings like Gochujang sticky buns and kimchi cheese danishes.

title: Stephen Curry swung by this North End restaurant while in town for Celtics-Warriors
author: John Waller
bullets: Stephen Curry visited Strega in Boston's North End ahead of the Warriors-Celtics game at TD Garden.
The Golden State Warriors star was seen at the popular restaurant despite being sidelined with a knee injury.
Curry's visit was highlighted on social media by the restaurant and host David Ledbury.

title: It’s finally payday for former Boston Sports Clubs members
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: The Massachusetts Attorney General's Office will distribute $4 million to former Boston Sports Clubs members improperly charged during COVID shutdowns.
A court found ex-CEO Patrick Walsh unlawfully billed members and violated the Health Club Act, impacting 47,000 consumers.
Boston Sports Club rebranded as New York Sports Club in 2023, now only three Boston locations remain.

title: Beat out by a hot tamale: Maine hosts the second-best specialty food festival in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Maine Whoopie Pie Festival in Dover-Foxcroft ranked No. 2 in USA Today's 2026 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards.
The Delta Hot Tamale Festival in Greenville, Miss. topped the list as the No. 1 specialty food festival.
The Maine festival on June 13 features whoopie pies, eating contests, crafts, and entertainment at $6 per person.

title: Man accused of recording people in South Shore library bathroom
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A Marshfield man, Thomas Campbell, 63, was arrested for allegedly recording people in a Cohasset library restroom.
Library staff at Paul Pratt Public Library found a hidden device, leading to Campbell's arrest for illegal wiretapping and video recording.
Campbell, held on $2,500 bail, will be arraigned Friday in Quincy District Court after at least one victim was identified.

title: Energy assistance runs out amid spiking oil prices
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Oil prices surged 30% amid geopolitical tensions, affecting affordability for heating oil in Massachusetts.
Action for Boston Community Development has provided fuel assistance to 170,000 households, with 70% reaching benefit limits.
The Healey administration raised maximum fuel assistance benefits to $1,400 due to rising oil costs.

title: Investing in quality: The benefits of well-made furniture and art
author: Regina Cole
bullets: Interior designer Kate Maloney emphasizes the value of locally made, quality furnishings that can be reused across different homes.
Custom-made pieces, like Tracy Glover's glass lighting fixtures, inspire design elements in Maloney's Cambridge project.
Maloney and art consultant Julie Mussafer advocate for investing in high-quality art to enhance home aesthetics.

title: ‘He drives them’: New Bruins forward Lukas Reichel made an instant impact at TD Garden
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lukas Reichel scored a goal and an assist in his debut for the Boston Bruins against the Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel was acquired from the Vancouver Canucks for a 2026 sixth-round pick, aiming to boost Bruins' third line.
Boston Bruins are in a tight race for Eastern Conference playoffs, tied at 84 points with Red Wings and Canadiens.

title: Ask the Remodeler: How to keep melted snow out of your basement
author: Mark Philben -- Globe Correspondent
bullets: Home improvement experts suggest removing snow from basement window wells to prevent water leakage during spring melts.
Regular gutter cleaning and installing extension pipes can help redirect water away from home foundations, reducing basement flooding risk.
Installing French drains or sump pumps provides long-term solutions to manage high water tables and prevent basement flooding.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston stories.
Peter Chianca is the general assignment editor at Boston.com since 2019.
Quiz aims to help residents stay informed on trending local news topics.
